GenoSim 1.0.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Introduction to GenoSim: Forward-Time Genotype Simulation

Compute Pairwise Linkage Disequilibrium

Source: R/analysis.R
compute_ld.Rd

Computes pairwise \(r^2\) and \(D'\) (normalised linkage disequilibrium) between all SNP pairs within a specified distance on the same chromosome. Fixed loci are skipped.

compute_ld(geno_matrix, snp_map, max_snps = 500L, max_dist_bp = 1e+06)

Arguments

geno_matrix

Integer dosage matrix \(n_{individuals} \times n_{SNPs}\).

snp_map

A data.frame with columns snp_id, chrom, and pos_bp.

max_snps

Integer. If ncol(geno_matrix) > max_snps, a random subsample is used. Default 500.

max_dist_bp

Numeric. Maximum inter-SNP distance in base pairs for which LD is computed. Default 1e6 (1 Mb).

Value

A data.frame with columns snp_a, snp_b, chrom, dist_bp, r2, and D_prime. Returns an empty data frame if no pairs qualify.

See also

plot_ld_decay, hwe_test

Examples

sim <- simulate_population(n_founders = 100, n_snps = 300,
                           n_generations = 2, chromosomes = 1:3, seed = 1)
#> === GenoSim: Population Forward Simulator ===
#>   Founders: 100 | SNPs: 300 | Generations: 2
#>   F=0.000 | mu=1.00e-04 | s=0.000 | Ne=100
#> [1/4] Simulating founder genotypes...
#> [2/4] Running forward simulation...
#>   -> Generation 1/2
#>   -> Generation 2/2
#> [3/4] Computing summary statistics...
#> 
#> === Simulation Complete ===
#>   Total individuals: 475 | SNPs: 300
ld  <- compute_ld(sim$genotypes[[1]], sim$snp_map, max_snps = 200)
#> LD: subsampled to 200 SNPs
hist(ld$r2, main = "r-squared distribution", xlab = "r-sq")
